research_note.summary: The first admitted tiny live LLM pilot completed one DeepSeek V4 Flash call and produced protocol-valid proposal-only export records. The run proves the live-recording boundary, not method quality.
what_worked: The admitted provider/model/config produced parseable JSON output in one non-streaming batch.
The raw source excerpt, prompt trace, provider request/response, and model trace remained in ignored local paths.
The committed records can carry model, prompt, source-hash, token, cost, and proposal-count metadata without committing raw source or provider payloads.
what_failed: No method success is claimed; method quality was not evaluated by this boundary review.
The committed record intentionally does not expose the full raw model output, so content-level review remains a future evaluation task.
negative_results: A successful provider call is not validation, strategy evidence, product evidence, financial advice, live-trading authority, or architecture.
Proposal counts are inspection metadata only and do not imply useful trading intelligence.
reusable_by_other_labs: Use proposal-only live records with explicit source hashes, prompt/config hashes, token/cost metadata, and trace-storage boundaries.
Keep full raw traces ignored until an explicit policy allows committing a sanitized review artifact.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat a live proposal export as a scaffold fixture.
Do not copy generated synthesis metrics or raw model text into currentness docs.
Do not substitute models silently when the admitted model is unavailable.
research_note.evidence_disclaimer: This is a proposal-only live LLM pilot record, not validation, product evidence, strategy evidence, financial advice, live-trading authority, or architecture.
